title: grevillea commutata subsp. commutata
intro: Grevillea commutata subsp. commutata, also known as the Bees' wings or Spider Flower, is a flowering plant native to Western Australia. The plant is a shrub that has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The leaves and flowers of the plant have been used in herbal preparations, such as infusions and tinctures, to harness its health benefits, including reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and promoting wound healing. Grevillea commutata subsp. commutata is also used to treat various health conditions, including colds, fever, and sore throats, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine.
